objective: Audit premium model policy, banned-model enforcement, Depth Guard, bounded spawning, and fallback handling for metaswarm readiness.

key_findings: Launcher supports --models rotation but only sets --model for commander pane; child enforcement is prompt/metadata-driven.
Default model mismatch between skill (Sonnet) and script (Haiku) can cause silent downgrade if script used directly.
Preflight only checks first model; CLI fallback to gh copilot is warning-only.
Banned model lists are consistent across commander schema/prose/capsule.
Enforcement is reactive (mark partial after fallback) with no pre-launch validation or retry guidance.
Depth-guard lint has no banned-model checks, allowing drift or overlap.
Docs align on no-silent-downgrade for full swarm failures.
Premium default is stated in README only; AGENTS lacks it.
Model default ambiguity between README and AGENTS.
Premium policy propagated via commander state/capsule and launcher metadata + prompt.
No env var export for policy; enforcement relies on prompt/state parsing.
Operator override language allows platform-refusal caveat.
Docs require partial when full swarm cannot launch; no silent downgrade.
No explicit handling for premium-model policy violations beyond swarm-size failure.

consensus_notes: All workers observed premium policy is largely instruction/metadata-driven with limited hard enforcement.
Multiple docs highlight no-silent-downgrade for swarm size but do not define premium-policy failure handling.
Lint coverage does not include banned-model or premium-policy consistency checks.
blockers: Task tool did not return explicit background agent IDs; launch_started events used child_id as agent-id surrogate.
